pages.raw_text:
Another market scheme was
sprung on the unsuspecting city
council last night. 1t is called the
Prairie Market and c. H. Reeves,
lumberman, is president The
company desires to erect the ne
cessary shelter on its property on
Washington st. between Pacific and
Second avs, charge for stall rents
and keep independent hucksters
away.


1t offers to pay the city 8 per
cent of their receipts and make set
tlement every month. The appli-
cation did not get as far as dis
cussion but was referred to the
market committee - Councilmen
Gray, Estep and Pratt.


The Central Labor union last
night formally protested that the
public market should be city in
stitution entirely and should not
be given into the control of private
parties.


The communication also recon-
mended a municipal employment
agency, and abolition of private
agencies on account of the man.
ner in which they rob laboring
men. The labor council is against
converting money from the water
fund to use in payment of other
city expenses. Day labor is advo-
Gated


The emergency hospital is favor
ed and also every assistance that
can be given to the inspector of
weights and measures. The COM
munication was referred to the
committee of the whole


The Moran Prairie grange COM
munication asking the council not
to transfer the proposed public
market to control of any corpora
ton was also referred to the COM
mittee.
